title: micromelum integerrimum
intro: Micromelum integerrimum, also known as the Thai grape or Chinese chestnut, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. The plant's bark, leaves, and fruits have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Micromelum integerrimum include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a garnish in salads.
